cards.definition: Official Coffee Board registration proving you're a legitimate exporter. Valid 3 years.
cards.analogy: Like a gym membership card, but for being a recognized exporter.
cards.why_it_matters: Without it you can't formally export; with it, customs and international buyers recognize you as legitimate.
cards.cloze: An ___ is the Coffee Board certificate that officially recognizes you as an exporter, valid for 3 years.

cards.definition: Required license to operate a curing works — the facility that turns raw cherry/parchment into clean, exportable green beans.
cards.analogy: Like an FSSAI license, but coffee-specific.
cards.why_it_matters: You won't run curing yourself, but it tells you the right question to ask toll roasters and curing works: 'are you licensed, and at what scale?'
cards.cloze: A ___ is required to operate a curing works that hulls, polishes, grades and sorts coffee into green beans.

cards.definition: Registration needed to run pulping equipment at scale — pulping strips skin and pulp off fresh cherry, usually within hours of picking.
cards.analogy: The very first processing step after picking.
cards.why_it_matters: If Shevaroys smallholders pulp their own cherry rather than selling wet cherry to a central point, that signals a more independent, quality-controlled farmer — a good storytelling detail.
cards.cloze: Pulping strips the skin off fresh cherry within hours of picking; running one at scale needs a ___.

cards.definition: Runs the weekly Wednesday–Thursday Bengaluru coffee auction — a public price-discovery mechanism.
cards.why_it_matters: ICTA prices are your best public benchmark for whether a farmer's asking price is fair, without seeming like you're haggling.
cards.cloze: ___ runs the weekly Bengaluru coffee auction that sets public benchmark prices like 'Plantation A' and 'Arabica cherry AB.'

cards.definition: The global body that tracks and reports coffee statistics across producing and consuming countries — source of the I-CIP price.
cards.analogy: Coffee's version of OPEC's reporting arm, but without price-setting power.
cards.why_it_matters: It only measures and reports — it doesn't set prices, unlike a cartel.
cards.cloze: The ___ is the global body that reports coffee statistics and publishes the I-CIP price, but does not set prices itself.
